extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property requires that setting pallor=true (all else unchanged) never lowers acuity. It is VIOLATED.

pallor appears in only one rule, r7 (priority 100): IF (vision_loss AND focal_weakness AND sweating AND hemoptysis AND syncope AND chest_pain AND palpitations AND vomiting AND confusion AND pallor) THEN acuity := self_care. self_care is the LOWEST acuity. r7 has priority 100, dominating every other rule (max other priority is 3).

Counterexample pair (back_pain held fixed at false; the 10 r7-conjuncts other than pallor all true):
- BEFORE (pallor=false): r7 does not fire (needs pallor). Firing rules include r2 (focal_weakness→emergent, pri 3), r4 (chest_pain→emergent, pri 3), r8 (vomiting→emergent, pri 3), r9/r10/r11 etc. Highest priority among firing rules = 3, setting acuity := emergent (highest).
- AFTER (pallor=true): all 10 conjuncts of r7 now hold, so r7 fires with priority 100, beating all priority-3 emergent rules. acuity := self_care (lowest).

Setting pallor true drops acuity from emergent to self_care, a strict decrease — directly violating the property. The witness_assignment is the after-state (pallor=true) input that, compared to its pallor=false counterpart, produces lower acuity.
